subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Article IV, OECD, WJP, RSF) + Japanese official (Statistics Bureau, Bank of Japan, IPSS, NPA) + Japanese press (Nikkei, Japan Times).
NEGATIVE-SKEW case: among the world's worst demographics (locked-in population decline) plus the highest major-economy debt - both worsen with horizon.
Source-lean: moderate Anglophone on political color and the China crisis; economic/demographic/seismic numbers come from Japanese/multilateral primaries.

Recalibrated 2026-05-29 for the 2026 Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, US+Israel vs Iran from 28 Feb) and the Strait of Hormuz closure: blockade of ~1/5 of world oil+LNG, attacks/threats on Gulf desalination (water security), and the global energy shock. The two-week 8 Apr ceasefire collapsed by late May - the US renewed strikes on Iran ~25-26 May (Trump convened Camp David) and the war resumed, with Hormuz still blockaded. (Reuters; BBC; NYT; CNN; ISW; Al Jazeera; CSIS; Wikipedia)

economic.notes: A credible central bank, a safe-haven reserve currency, and an export-tech base, against the highest major-economy debt and a debt-service cost that worsens as rates normalize.

fiscal_state.notes: Gross debt ~248% GDP (highest of major economies) but ~90% domestically/BoJ-held with low near-term rollover risk; the IMF warns debt-service cost roughly doubles by 2030 as rates rise - the long horizon worsens sharply. (IMF 2026 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: A genuinely independent Bank of Japan, normalizing policy after decades of ultra-loose settings. (BoJ)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The yen is a major reserve currency (~6% of reserves) and a traditional safe haven; debt and demographics erode the long horizon. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: Near-total reliance on seaborne energy makes Japan acutely exposed to the 2026 Hormuz oil/LNG shock. (Reuters)

banking_stability.notes: The IMF judges the system broadly resilient; the watch-item is rising JGB unrealized losses as rates climb. (IMF FSAP; BoJ FSR)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A high-tech/auto/machinery export power with stable policy and no resource curse, though heavily energy-import dependent; US auto tariffs a near-term drag. (Nikkei; Japan Times)

institutional.notes: Strong courts, credible statistics, and high state capacity; press freedom mediocre for a democracy.

statistical_integrity.notes: Credible; the 2018-19 labour-stats scandal was investigated and resolved. (Statistics Bureau)

civil_service_capacity.notes: High competence and continuity; slow digitalization. (OECD)

press_freedom.notes: Mediocre for a democracy (RSF ~66): the press-club (kisha) system, self-censorship, and advertiser pressure. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: Restored single-party dominance and very high social order, against a low immigration ceiling and a locked-in demographic collapse.

political_stability.notes: After 2024-25 minority-government instability and PM turnover, a Feb 2026 snap election gave the LDP a supermajority; stability restored but reliant on one polarizing leader. (Brookings; Carnegie)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful, expanding labor channels (Highly Skilled Professional and Specified Skilled Worker visas), but capped, with the language barrier and limited path to belonging real ceilings; a 2025 deportation drive. Open for skilled labor, low belonging. (OECD Migration Outlook 2025)

social_cohesion.notes: Very high social order and low crime, but rising anti-immigrant politics and reported crime up for a third straight year. (Japan Times)

demographics.notes: Among the world's worst: population ~123M and falling, ~29% aged 65+, fertility ~1.15, fewer than 670k births in 2025 (a record low). Locked-in negative skew. (IPSS; Statistics Bureau)

geopolitical.notes: A core US treaty ally, rearming, with an elevated regional-conflict tail (China/Taiwan/North Korea).

alliance_reliability.notes: A core US treaty ally, rearming to 2% of GDP early, with a next-gen fighter program. (CRS)

conflict_involvement.notes: An active 2025-26 China-Japan diplomatic crisis (PLA drills, a seafood ban, Senkaku advisories) plus North Korean missiles - an elevated regional tail risk. (ORF)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Free capital mobility; aligned with G7 sanctions, no domestic controls. (observed)

physical_practical.notes: World-class infrastructure, healthcare, and very low crime, against the standout seismic/tsunami risk (offset by world-best preparedness).

climate.notes: Resilient/high-capacity but with real exposure - intensifying typhoons, extreme heat, sea-level on dense coasts. (IPCC AR6; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Universal coverage with world-leading longevity and outcomes; ageing-driven cost strain. (OECD)

crime_safety.notes: A genuine strength: homicide ~0.78/100k, among the lowest globally. (NPA)

disaster_insurance.notes: Seismic is the standout physical risk (a Nankai Trough megaquake ~80% within 30 years), netted against the world's best preparedness (codes, drills, quake-insurance system, capable state response). (Cabinet Office)

summary: Strong courts and state capacity, world-class infrastructure and healthcare, very low crime, a credible central bank, and a safe-haven reserve currency now exiting deflation. But the outcome distribution skews negative: among the world's worst demographics (a locked-in population decline) and the highest major-economy debt (with debt-service costs worsening as rates normalize), a low immigration ceiling, mediocre press freedom, and an elevated regional-conflict tail. Seismic risk is the standout physical hazard, offset by world-best preparedness.
